commit.message:
fix(macos): fix clipboard copy failing from tray and GUI (#4629)

The clipboard copy from tray menu and capture GUI was broken on macOS:

1. FlameshotDaemon::copyToClipboard() created a KDSingleApplication
   instance and called isPrimaryInstance(), which always returned false
   (the daemon already holds the socket). This caused a 3-second IPC
   timeout before falling back. Fix: use instance() to check if the
   daemon singleton exists in-process, matching non-macOS behavior.

2. saveToClipboardMime() used setData("image/...") which puts a lazy
   reference on the macOS pasteboard. If the app exits before paste,
   data is lost ("Cannot keep promise"). Fix: use setImageData() for
   native pasteboard persistence, plus setData() for exact format.

3. saveJpegToClipboardMacOS() used osascript with a temp file and
   incorrectly labeled JPEG data as PNG UTI. Removed entirely — the
   fixed saveToClipboardMime() now handles both JPEG and PNG.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove redundant spaCy download step from deploy workflow

The spaCy model wheel URLs are already in requirements.txt, so
pip install -r requirements.txt --target ... installs them.
The separate python -m spacy download step fails because spaCy
isn't on the system Python path (it's in .python_packages/).

Also granted Storage Blob Data Contributor to the service principal
for blob uploads.

the buttons are disabled immediately (preventing double-press) and the card
stays in the DOM. Removal is now driven entirely by a backend SSE delete event

if the POST itself fails.

commit.message:
contracts: fix §1 estimate ref, new §2 penalty clauses, configurable penalty UI

- §1: uses estimate number (Kosztorysem nr WY-XXX) instead of address/name
- §2: replaced old single-clause with 4-point structure per user's legal text:
  - point 2: max 30-day extension for unforeseen circumstances
  - point 3: automatic extension for investor-caused delays
  - point 4: penalty only after extended deadline, with configurable rate
- ContractForm: new 'Kary umowne' section with per-day % and max % inputs
  - live preview of penalty clause text before saving
  - defaults: 0.1% per day, max 10%
- model.ts: added penalty_per_day_pct and max_penalty_pct optional fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(dev-tools): converge dev runtime into unified 09-dev-tools layer (#47)

* feat(dev-tools): converge dev runtime into unified 09-dev-tools layer

Create the canonical dev-tools module (src/dev-tools/) that establishes a
single authoritative development-tooling layer for file watching, rebuild
triggering, hot reload orchestration, and lifecycle events.

New components:
- FileWatcher: canonical file watcher with debouncing, filtering, batch
  merging, and path management (extracted from dev-session inline chokidar)
- HotReloadOrchestrator: wraps HotReloadClient with before/after hooks,
  sequence tracking, and concurrent reload protection
- DevServer: unified dev runtime facade composing FileWatcher +
  BuildPipeline + HotReloadOrchestrator with full state machine and event
  system

Architecture:
- DevServer is the new canonical dev API (createDevServer)
- DevSession refactored to compose the same dev-tools primitives
  (FileWatcher, HotReloadOrchestrator) for backward compatibility
- Both paths share the same unified orchestration components
- executeDev/dev-command continues to work through DevSession unchanged

Tests:
- 10 FileWatcher tests (FW-01 through FW-10)
- 12 HotReloadOrchestrator tests (HR-01 through HR-12)
- 15 DevServer tests (DV-01 through DV-15)
- All 29 existing DevSession tests pass unmodified
- All 3 existing dev-command tests pass unmodified
- Full suite: 1513 pass, 0 fail, 1 skip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dev-tools): address foundation review feedback

* refactor(dev-tools): make DevSession a thin DevServer wrapper

---------

commit.message:
macb: Circle-matched init, RX descriptor diag, BAR2 firmware preserve

- MACB init rewritten to match Circle's macb.cpp sequence:
  - DMACFG read-modify-write preserving firmware defaults
  - RXBMS=3 (max), TXPBMS=1, FBLDO=16
  - TX addr=0 during init (set during send, Circle pattern)
  - NCR: TE|RE only, MPE toggled per MDIO operation
  - Minimal NCFGR with DBW from DCFG1 before PHY, full after negotiation
- MACB RX: volatile raw pointer read + DSB/ISB for DMA visibility
  - Periodic descriptor dump (raw[0], raw[1], RSR, RBQP)
  - Clears RSR to track new BNA events
- PCIe: preserve firmware BAR2 config if already programmed
  - Log firmware BAR2_LO/HI/REMAP before any modification

commit.message:
Fix BearSSL build: use system gcc instead of musl-native

The musl-native toolchain (x86_64-linux-musl-native) is linked against
musl libc and cannot execute on Ubuntu CI runners (glibc). This caused
the build step to hang.

Fix: use system gcc with -ffreestanding -fno-builtin. The output .o
files have zero libc dependency, making them compatible with any linker
including the musl linker shipped in release bundles.

Also removed the Python/toolchain-fetch steps since they're no longer
needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 'Open app' intent link from sourcePackage field

When a notification includes sourcePackage (e.g. com.google.android.deskclock),
a small pill-shaped '📲 Open app' link is shown below the body. The href uses
the Android intent:// URI scheme which Chrome on Android follows to launch the
originating app directly:
  intent://#Intent;package=<pkg>;action=android.intent.action.MAIN;
          category=android.intent.category.LAUNCHER;end

On desktop browsers the link does nothing harmful (no app to open).
This covers the missing intent on the 'Klocka' (Clock) notification.

commit.message:
feat: advanced tutorial notebooks, @algo API, EPEX/validation fixes — release 0.4.0b1 (#14)

* Add advanced tutorial notebooks: @algo API and custom signals

Add two new Jupyter notebooks covering the advanced concepts not
addressed by the existing walkthroughs:

- notebooks/algo_decorator_walkthrough.ipynb
  Covers the @algo async event-stream API: event type hierarchy,
  building a DA price logger, an IDC spread scalper (step-by-step),
  gate closure management, and signals via ctx.get_signal().
  Includes visualisations of the event timeline and backtest PnL.

- notebooks/custom_signals_walkthrough.ipynb
  Covers implementing the SignalProvider protocol: DataFrameSignalProvider
  from scratch, publication_offset semantics with a timeline chart,
  signal history lookback, get_delivery_position(), and gate closure
  NOP analysis.

Also updates README.md to replace the single notebook link with a table
of all four notebooks in suggested reading order.

All four notebooks execute end-to-end without errors (make execute-notebooks).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IDC VWAP benchmarking, GateClosureWarning, and equity curve accuracy

- Compute per-product IDC market VWAPs incrementally from HistoricalTrade
  events (O(P) memory, compatible with sliding-window replay); each fill
  is now benchmarked against its own product's market VWAP rather than a
  single portfolio-level number, giving accurate win/loss attribution
- Emit GateClosureWarning to the @algo event stream one MTU before gate
  closure for each IDC product, giving the algo a last chance to act
- Equity curve snapshots now use running per-product VWAPs instead of
  zero, making the intra-run curve directionally meaningful
- Extract _dispatch_signals() helper to eliminate duplicated signal loop
  between DA and IDC paths
- Update notebooks and tests to match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 0.4.0b1 and update implementation status

- Bump _version.py to 0.4.0b1
- Update README implementation status table to mark @algo, EPEX SPOT,
  gate closure NOP tracking, and validation pipeline as Done

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(eval): multi-turn conversation mode with turn-by-turn evaluation (#1054)

* feat(eval): add multi-turn conversation mode with turn-by-turn evaluation

Implements issue #1052: support for evaluating multi-turn conversations
where the agent generates each assistant turn with per-turn grading.

- Add ConversationTurn type, mode, turns, aggregation, on_turn_failure, window_size to EvalTest
- Zod schema and YAML parser updates for new fields
- Turn-by-turn loop in orchestrator: accumulate messages, call provider, grade, repeat
- Conversation assertions run after all turns
- Aggregation: mean (default), min (weakest-link), max
- String shorthand in per-turn assertions works identically to top-level
- Cross-field validation (turns requires mode:conversation, etc.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(eval): add multi-turn conversation-live example for UAT

Adds examples/features/multi-turn-conversation-live/ with 5 test cases
exercising conversation mode features: context retention, aggregation
modes, on_turn_failure, mixed assertions, and conversation-level assertions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(eval): add unit tests for multi-turn conversation mode

Tests for conversation-mode orchestrator, validation rules,
and score aggregation (mean/min/max).

Also fixes buildTurnAssertions to emit type: 'llm-grader' with rubrics
instead of type: 'rubrics' (which is not registered in the builtin registry).
The evaluator-parser uses the same pattern for YAML-sourced rubrics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eval): correct conversation mode scoring, loader, and serialization

- YAML loader: include `turns` in completeness gate so conversation-only
  cases (no top-level criteria/assertions) are not silently skipped
- Orchestrator: stop falling back to evalCase.assertions per-turn — turns
  without own assertions score 1.0 instead of double-counting top-level
- Orchestrator: pass full transcript as candidate for conversation-level
  grading instead of only the last assistant reply
- Orchestrator: serialize structured message content with JSON.stringify
  instead of producing [object Object] in transcript strings
- Validator: reject whitespace-only and empty-array turn inputs
- Tests: add regression coverage for double-counting, transcript candidate,
  and whitespace input validation

commit.message:
feat(#209): replace JSON schema injection with native LiteRT-LM ToolSet API (#210)

* feat(#209): replace JSON schema injection with native LiteRT-LM ToolSet API

FunctionGemma is fine-tuned to use the SDK's @Tool/@ToolParam/ToolSet
mechanism, not manual JSON in the system prompt. Previous approach caused
the model to respond "I don't have a tool" for every skill call because
tools were never registered with the SDK.

- Create KernelAIToolSet implementing ToolSet with @Tool-annotated methods
  for flashlight (on/off), weather, timer, saveMemory
- Rewrite FunctionGemmaRouter.initialize() to pass ToolProvider via
  ConversationConfig(tools = ...) instead of injecting JSON schema
- Update system prompt to match Edge Gallery reference: "model that can
  do function calling" + current date/time (no manual declarations)
- Replace route() returning RouteDecision sealed class with handle()
  returning String? — SDK calls tools natively; we get final text back
- Update ChatViewModel to use handle() and remove RouteDecision handling
- Remove buildSkillContext() and functionDeclarationsJson parameter from
  initialize()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(#209): add HandleResult sealed class and wasToolCalled() for tool-vs-conversation routing

Distinguishes between:
- ToolHandled: a @Tool method was invoked → display FunctionGemma response, skip Gemma-4
- PlainResponse: FunctionGemma answered but called no tool → fall through to Gemma-4
- NotReady: router not initialised → fall through to Gemma-4

This prevents conversational queries ("what's the capital of France?") from being
answered by FunctionGemma's weaker plain-text response instead of Gemma-4.

KernelAIToolSet: @Volatile toolCalledInThisTurn flag, resetTurnState(), wasToolCalled()
FunctionGemmaRouter: HandleResult sealed class, handle() returns HandleResult
ChatViewModel: when(HandleResult) replaces null-check

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#209): address code review — close prev resources on re-init, redact memory log, fix empty-response routing

- FunctionGemmaRouter.initialize(): close previous Engine+Conversation before
  re-initialising to prevent ~270MB native memory leak on second call
- KernelAIToolSet.saveMemory(): log content.length only, not content (PII)
- FunctionGemmaRouter.handle(): empty non-tool response returns PlainResponse("")
  instead of NotReady — correctly reflects router is ready, model returned nothing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace painterly/oil-painting style prompt with bold, high-contrast
graphic editorial illustration matching The Economist's actual visual
identity. Update both source files, tests, skill docs, and related
documentation references.

Closes #137

commit.message:
GPM-46: warn on broken relationship symmetry during cache sync

- Add internal/cache/validate.go with validateRelationshipSymmetry()
  - Checks depends-on ↔ blocks pairs
  - Checks related ↔ related pairs (deduped, reported once per pair)
  - Each warning includes pm link fix command on second line
- Hook into SyncCache after scanTicketFiles; print to stderr (non-fatal)
- Unit tests: 7 cases covering all warning types and edge cases
- Integration tests: 3 tests covering depends-on, related, and clean data

commit.message:
Add description, category, and chart presence checks to PublicationValidator (#247)

* Initial plan

* feat: add description, category, and chart presence checks to PublicationValidator

Adds three new Economist content baseline checks:
- _check_description(): validates description front matter present and ≤160 chars
- _check_category(): validates categories against 4 valid blog categories
- _check_chart_presence(): warns when no /assets/charts/ image referenced

Updates VALID_CATEGORIES in MCP server to match.
Adds 11 new unit tests covering all new checks.
Fixes test_references_feature.py to include required description field.
